criteria.name: memory/context-binding subsuite passed
module-state/routing subsuite passed
context-memory shams lose somewhere on memory pressure
context-memory beats sign persistence on memory pressure
routing shams lose on delayed module-state pressure
routing beats routing-off CRA on delayed module-state pressure
full variant/baseline/control/task/seed matrix completed
feedback timing has no leakage violations
candidate context feature is active
candidate memory receives context updates
candidate reaches minimum accuracy on capacity-interference tasks
keyed candidate improves over v1.4 raw CRA
keyed candidate improves over v1.5 single-slot memory
keyed candidate approaches oracle-key scaffold
criteria.note: Internal keyed memory can trail the oracle-key upper bound slightly but cannot collapse relative to it.

criteria.name: full keyed memory is not worse than overcapacity keyed control
criteria.note: Overcapacity control documents graceful degradation when slots are too few.

criteria.name: memory ablations are worse than candidate
candidate beats sign persistence
candidate is competitive with best standard baseline
criteria.note: Strong baselines may still win some tasks, but candidate cannot be far behind before promotion.

tier_summary.claim_boundary: Software stress diagnostic only: internal host-side context binding under capacity/interference pressure, not native on-chip memory, sleep/replay, or hardware evidence.

criteria.name: routing task/variant/baseline/seed matrix completed

routing mechanism receives context-router updates
routing mechanism selects features before feedback
candidate reaches routing first-heldout threshold
candidate reaches routing heldout threshold
candidate route selection is correct
candidate improves over routing-off CRA
routing shams are worse than candidate
candidate does not underperform selected sequence baselines

summary.claim_boundary: Software diagnostic only: v1.9 host-side context memory and module routing under working-memory pressure; not hardware/on-chip, language, planning, or AGI evidence.
